title: Messi and Mbappé tied at 8 goals
description: Through July 14, 2026, Lionel Messi (Argentina) and Kylian Mbappé (France) are tied for the tournament goal-scoring lead with 8 goals each — the closest Golden Boot race in recent World Cups.

title: Highest attendance in World Cup history
description: Total attendance passed 3,605,357 on June 25, 2026, breaking the record set by the 1994 USA World Cup — driven by the expanded 48-team, three-nation format.

title: First 48-team, three-host World Cup
description: 2026 is the first FIFA World Cup with 48 teams (up from 32) and the first jointly hosted by three nations — Canada, Mexico, and the United States.

title: Mexico open the tournament with a win
description: Co-hosts Mexico beat South Africa 2-0 in the opening match at Estadio Azteca, Mexico City, on June 11, 2026 — the first match of the expanded tournament.

title: 294 goals across 101 matches
description: As of July 14, 2026, the tournament has produced 294 goals in 101 matches — an average of 2.91 goals per game.
